element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Plot device separating normal world from combat arena
Advanced combat mechanic requiring specific trigger conditions
Returns to original world as final challenge

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Fast-paced fighting with small sprite sizes compensated by rapid game speed and responsive controls

spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Blood code accessible via hidden button sequence (similar to Mortal Kombat tradition)

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction to basic fighting mechanics in dojo setting with Leotsu as tutorial opponent
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Progression through Second World with increasing minion difficulty and varied enemy types
difficulty_curve.late_game: Boss encounter with Sett-Ra in Second World, followed by rematch with Beast in original world
notable_spikes: Sett-Ra boss fight (evil mummy with advanced movesets)
Beast rematch (returns from Second World with revenge motivation)

notable_level_patterns.description: Safe training environment in Tokyo dojo where player meets Leotsu and learns mechanics before dimensional transport
Tournament-style advancement through alternate dimension with escalating enemy difficulty leading to Sett-Ra boss
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Second World stages (Genesis version has additional stages: The Lab, The Wasteland, Yasko Mines)

notable_level_patterns.description: Returns to original world setting with Beast encounter during basketball game context

notes: T3 Data Tier - Source provides plot structure and character introduction order, limited mechanical rhythm data. SNES version has 7 playable characters and fewer stages than Genesis version (5 additional characters, 3 additional stages). No detailed frame-by-frame pacing data available in source.
